judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with equivalent, well-grounded detail; they are comparable.

report_1.evidence: Engineer A — pipeline/registry.py: F-05 (bump DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION...), F-09 (fix DEFAULT_DATASET...), and F-02 (add the aurora-1 mapping to MODEL_ENDPOINTS...) ... These are three one-line edits to a 12-line file; bundling them into one commit by one person eliminates guaranteed merge conflicts.

report_2.evidence: registry.py is 11 lines with three findings (F-02, F-05, F-09) — same reasoning as run_batch.py, keep with one engineer.

report_1.evidence: Wave 1 — four engineers fully in parallel... Engineer B — pipeline/rubric.py: F-11 first... Engineer A — pipeline/registry.py: F-05..., F-09..., and F-02 ... bundling them into one commit by one person

report_2.evidence: Wave 1 (all in parallel, day one): A's whole stream, B's whole stream, C's F-05/F-09, D's F-10... Engineer C — pipeline/registry.py: F-05... and F-09... immediately; F-02 ... only once the real endpoint URL is obtained... Engineer B — pipeline/rubric.py: F-01 ... together with F-11 (the edge-case parsing test)

report_1.evidence: F-06 — escalate, don't assign. "Too lenient; tighten them" specifies no target values, and changing PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS (score.py:3) changes the scores of every borderline answer the pipeline will ever grade — a scoring-policy decision, not an engineering one. Batch it to the pipeline owner for concrete thresholds

report_2.evidence: F-06 — a policy decision, not a bug fix. "Tighten the thresholds so borderline answers score lower" names no target values. PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS = (0.35, 0.65) ... determines every downstream score, so changing it silently breaks comparability... This needs the rubric owner to choose and sign off on values

report_1.evidence: A private RESULTS_DB. Export RESULTS_DB to a per-worktree temp path before running tests, because tests/conftest.py:4 otherwise defaults everyone to the shared /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db. ... Several engineers running the suite concurrently on one machine would stomp each other's results database unless each overrides RESULTS_DB.

report_2.evidence: tests/conftest.py:4 defaults RESULTS_DB to the machine-global path /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db; engineers running tests on a shared box would share that file unless each overrides the env var. ... Set RESULTS_DB ... to a per-engineer scratch path; the default ... is shared machine-wide.
